page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 family. It is equipped with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance runs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz, consisting of 1 physical core and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. Cache architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities paired with limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng benchmark scores 1398.19, and the Geekbench single-core score reaches 1123.0, both placing in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is constrained by the two-vCPU limit, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1333.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1837.38, both falling into the lower percentile. Memory bandwidth is also modest, measuring 13.8 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 11.79 GB/sec for write operations. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES, though overall database and web serving throughput remain low, with Redis SET operations averaging 1.72 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 1.95 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-2 offers a predictable, dedicated resource profile suitable for entry-level workloads without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. Because it lacks local storage and high-performance multi-core throughput, it is best utilized for lightweight general-purpose applications. Appropriate workloads include small-scale web serving, basic utility scripting, development environments, and low-traffic application backends that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory bandwidth or parallel processing power.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz, it features 1 physical core and 2 threads. The hardware profile includes 33 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1123.0, alongside lower-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to its dual-vCPU configuration. This instance offers a qualitative balance of dedicated resources for entry-level workloads. It is best suited for lightweight applications, small-scale web serving, development environments, and basic background tasks that require predictable CPU allocation without the performance variability of burstable instances.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, offering dedicated resources for lightweight workloads and development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine with a balanced memory profile designed for lightweight workloads and predictable entry-level compute performance.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM on a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor.
Delivers stable single-core performance with a 2.8 GHz clock speed and AVX-512 support.
Configured without local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic background tasks.
